title: Add podman-clean-transient.service service
body:
This is a unit that can be enabled when using transient store mode to clean up potential leftovers from previous boots. All it does is run "podman system prune --external" once each boot.

Signed-off-by: Alexander Larsson <alexl@redhat.com>

```release-note
Added a systemd unit file that is useful for transient storage mode cleanup
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *alexlarsson*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Code LGTM As for docs, this new unit file is not mentioned in any documentation file. How will users know to use it?

For now, there's only one user :^)  I think the unit file is too restricted to one use case to be documented outside the auto use case.  I may be wrong in the future but if we add docs, we should mention the limitations.

I think the use case is for edge systems in general.  But we need to make some of this more discoverable.
/lgtm
I will merge for now, but I think we need to get quadlet and transient more into the main readme's and man pages.
